title: Prove Ledger recoverability
description: Backup, independent verification, separate-target restore, and supported storage evolution must preserve the complete authoritative financial state.
target: Every accepted recovery drill reproduces the complete Durable Ledger State, including metadata, accounts, categories, transactions/correction chains, allocation history, active/retired relationships, idempotency outcomes, per-type counts, relationship rules, and exact derived totals; every failed drill leaves the previous Ledger authoritative.
load_condition: A representative acceptance Ledger containing every durable entity and history type, identical and conflicting idempotency outcomes, active and retired relationships, and the supported maximum version distance.
measurement: Automated backup-verify-restore and upgrade/migration drills compare normalized complete-state manifests and derived totals, with corruption, omission, permission, disk-space, compatibility, and activation fault cases.
rationale: A backup is useful only when restoration and evolution are independently proven. Supports G8.
